title: Fertilizer Access And Soil Nutrient Depletion In Smallholder Agriculture In Sub-Saharan Africa
summary: Build a verified workflow for mapping fertilizer access gaps and soil nutrient depletion at sub-national scale in SSA smallholder farming systems, with explicit fertilizer-use data limitations, distribution-infrastructure barriers, and soil-specific response variability documentation.
why_it_matters: SSA smallholder farmers use 15 kg/ha of fertilizer — one-tenth the global average. Soil nutrient depletion costs 3 percent of agricultural GDP annually. Fertilizer distribution is concentrated near transport corridors while remote areas face 50-200 percent price markups. Fertilizer response in degraded soils is 30-50 percent lower than in well-managed soils. Without verified sub-national analysis, fertilizer subsidy and soil health programs cannot be targeted where they would most improve food security.

success_metrics.target: Every fertilizer-access claim documents whether data is national aggregate or sub-national, and explicitly states where district-level data is absent.
success_metrics.validation_method: Schema validation and reviewer spot-checks against fertilizer-use data documentation.

success_metrics.target: No fertilizer-access analysis is merged without documenting distribution-infrastructure barriers including transport costs, price markups, and rural availability.
success_metrics.validation_method: Reviewer confirms distribution-infrastructure barrier documentation for each analyzed country.

success_metrics.target: No fertilizer-effectiveness claim is merged without documenting that response rates in degraded soils are 30-50 percent lower and that soil-specific recommendations are essential.
success_metrics.validation_method: Red-team reviewer confirms soil-response variability documentation for every effectiveness claim.

review_policy.merge_gate: Schema validation, domain review, soil-response safety review, and replication for quantitative outputs.
